title: Prospect Research & ICP Profiling
description: Research 10 target accounts, build Ideal Customer Profile (ICP) criteria, and create a prioritised prospect list with key intelligence for each account. This is the foundation of all outbound selling — before you can sell, you need to know who to sell to and why they'd care.
whyItMatters: Every wasted hour spent on a bad-fit prospect is an hour not spent on a deal that could close. The best AEs are ruthless about who they spend time on. Building a strong ICP and doing deep account research before you reach out separates strategic sellers from spray-and-pray reps. This project teaches you the discipline of targeted prospecting.
skillsLearned: Account research using LinkedIn, company websites, and news
Identifying trigger events and buying signals

steps.title: Define Your ICP
steps.description: Choose a product or service (real or hypothetical) and define the Ideal Customer Profile. Document: company size (revenue and headcount), industry/vertical, geography, technology stack signals, common pain points, typical buyer titles, and disqualification criteria (who is NOT a fit).
steps.tips: Start with your best customers (or hypothetical best customers) and work backwards. What do they have in common? Be specific — 'Australian SaaS companies with 50-200 employees, using Salesforce, in growth mode' is better than 'tech companies.'

steps.title: Build Your Target Account List
steps.description: Using LinkedIn Sales Navigator, Apollo.io, or manual research, identify 10 companies that match your ICP. For each, record: company name, website, headcount, estimated revenue, industry, and why they match your ICP.
steps.tips: Look for companies showing buying signals: recent funding rounds, new executive hires, job postings for roles your product supports, technology adoption signals, or expansion announcements.

steps.title: Research Each Account
steps.description: For each of the 10 accounts, create a one-page intelligence brief. Include: company overview, recent news, key executives (with LinkedIn profiles), technology stack (use BuiltWith or Wappalyzer), potential pain points, and a personalisation hook for outreach.
steps.tips: Spend 10-15 minutes per account. Check their LinkedIn company page, recent press releases, Glassdoor reviews (for internal culture signals), and job postings (which reveal priorities and gaps).

steps.title: Score and Prioritise
steps.description: Create a simple scoring model to rank your 10 accounts. Score each on: ICP fit (1-5), buying signals present (1-5), accessibility of contacts (1-5), and estimated deal size (1-5). Total the scores and rank from highest to lowest. Your top 3-4 accounts are where you should focus first.
steps.tips: Don't overthink the scoring — the goal is to force a prioritisation decision, not to build a perfect model. A simple spreadsheet with four columns works perfectly.

deliverables: A written ICP document with clear inclusion and exclusion criteria
A spreadsheet of 10 target accounts with company details and ICP match rationale
One-page intelligence briefs for each of the 10 accounts
A scored and ranked priority list with your top 3-4 accounts identified

rubric.description: ICP is specific enough to filter out bad-fit accounts. Includes company size, industry, geography, pain points, and disqualification criteria.
Intelligence briefs go beyond basic company info — they include trigger events, key contacts, technology stack, and personalisation hooks.
A clear, logical scoring model is applied consistently across all 10 accounts, leading to a defensible prioritisation.
The output is genuinely useful for outreach — you could hand this to an SDR and they'd have enough context to start personalised prospecting immediately.
